questions.text: Enzymes lower the activation energy by stabilizing the transition state of a metabolic reaction due to?

questions.text: In which of the following functional groups, the carbon atom is sp hybridized?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: In the —CHO functional group, the carbon atom is sp² hybridized. It forms a sigma bond with hydrogen (H) and a double bond with oxygen (O).

In the —COOH functional group, the carbon atom is also sp² hybridized. It forms a sigma bond with hydrogen (H), a double bond with oxygen (O), and a single bond with another oxygen (O) in the carboxyl group.

In the —CN functional group, the carbon atom is sp hybridized. This means that the carbon atom in this group has two sigma bonds and no lone pairs of electrons around it. The sp hybridization allows the carbon atom to form a strong triple bond with nitrogen (N), resulting in a linear molecular geometry. This functional group is called a nitrile or cyanide group.
In the —COOR functional group, the carbon atom is sp² hybridized. It forms a sigma bond with hydrogen (H), a double bond with oxygen (O), and a single bond with the alkyl group (R).

questions.text: When halogen is removed from an alkyl halide a carbocation is formed, identify the most reactive carbocation
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Primary Carbocation (1°)Structure: The positively charged carbon is bonded to one alkyl group.Stability: More stable than methyl, but still quite unstable.Reactivity: Less reactive than methyl, but more reactive than secondary and tertiary.Reason: Slight stabilization from one alkyl group via inductive effect and a little hyperconjugation.

Secondary Carbocation (2°)Structure: Positively charged carbon bonded to two alkyl groups.Stability: Moderately stable.Reactivity: Lower reactivity than primary and methyl.Reason: More hyperconjugation and inductive effects compared to primary.

Tertiary Carbocation (3°)Structure: Carbon bearing the positive charge is attached to three alkyl groups.Stability: Most stable due to maximum hyperconjugation and inductive donation from three neighboring alkyl groups.Reactivity: Least reactive in the general sense, because it’s already quite stable.Reason: It has minimal driving force to undergo further reaction — it doesn’t need to stabilize further.

Methyl Carbocation (CH₃⁺)Structure: Only has one carbon atom, with no alkyl groups to stabilize the positive charge.Stability: Least stable of all carbocations.Reactivity: Most reactive, because it desperately seeks stabilization.Reason: No hyperconjugation or inductive effect; the positive charge sits entirely on one carbon with no support.

questions.explanation: Methyl Carbocation (CH₃⁺)Structure: Only has one carbon atom, with no alkyl groups to stabilize the positive charge.Stability: Least stable of all carbocations.Reactivity: Most reactive, because it desperately seeks stabilization.Reason: No hyperconjugation or inductive effect; the positive charge sits entirely on one carbon with no support.

questions.text: What is the shape of velocity-time graph for constant acceleration?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Parabola line: A parabola occurs when acceleration changes over time (non-constant acceleration), not with constant acceleration.

In the case of constant acceleration, the velocity-time graph will be a straight line. The slope of this line represents the acceleration. If the slope is positive, it indicates positive acceleration (increasing velocity), while a negative slope represents negative acceleration (decreasing velocity). The steeper the slope, the greater the acceleration.
Incline curve: An incline curve suggests varying acceleration, but with constant acceleration, the graph is a straight line, not curved.
Decline curve: A decline curve would indicate decreasing velocity with varying acceleration, which is not the case with constant acceleration.

When a stone is thrown horizontally from the top of a tall building, it experiences only the force of gravity acting vertically downward. Since there is no initial vertical velocity, the stone's motion is governed solely by the downward force of gravity. The path of the stone is a result of the combination of its horizontal velocity (due to the initial throw) and the vertical acceleration due to gravity. As the stone falls vertically, it also continues to move horizontally due to its initial horizontal velocity. The resulting trajectory of the stone is a curved path known as a parabola.

questions.text: A fighter plane is moving in a vertical circle of radius r. Its minimum velocity at the highest point of the circle will be?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: At the highest point, the centripetal force is provided by the vertical component of the plane's weight, given by: Fc = mg Equating this to the centripetal force formula: mv²/r = mg Cancelling the mass and rearranging the equation: v² = gr Taking the square root of both sides: v = √gr Therefore, the minimum velocity at the highest point of the vertical circle is √gr.

questions.text: The enzyme ATP synthase is located in the membrane of?

options.explanation: The ATP synthases comprise a very large group of highly conserved enzymes that are found in the bacterial cytoplasmic membranes, the thylakoid membranes of chloroplasts, and the inner membranes of mitochondria.

They contain a variety of hydrolytic enzymes that function optimally in the acidic environment within lysosomes.

ATP synthase is not typically found in the membrane of the Golgi apparatus. The Golgi apparatus is an organelle involved in modifying, sorting, and packaging proteins and lipids for transport to their final destinations within the cell or for secretion outside the cell.The Golgi apparatus consists of a series of flattened membrane-bound compartments called cisternae. It has a distinct structural organization with a cis face (forming the entry side) and a trans face (forming the exit side). Proteins and lipids move through the Golgi apparatus from the cis face to the trans face as they undergo various processing steps.

ATP synthase is not found in the membrane of the nucleus. The nucleus is a membrane-bound organelle that contains the cell's genetic material, including DNA and RNA. Its primary function is to regulate gene expression and control cellular activities.ATP synthase is located in membranes that are directly involved in energy production, such as the inner mitochondrial membrane or the thylakoid membrane in chloroplasts.

options.explanation: Synapsis is the pairing of homologous chromosomes before their separation into daughter cells. Hence, this option is incorrect.
The places where neurons connect and communicate with each other are called synapses. They are the microscopic gaps between two neurons. Hence, this option is the correct option.
There is no such term in biology. Hence, this option is incorrect.

A synaptic knob is a terminal branch at the end of the presynaptic neuron. The presynaptic neuron ends in a swelling and is called the synaptic knob. It is not the microscopic gap between two neurons, so the option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The places where neurons connect and communicate with each other, and have a microscopic gap, are called synapses. Hence, option B is the correct option.

questions.text: The feelings and emotions of love and hate are controlled by _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The amygdala is a part of the limbic system that deals with emotions and feelings, such as anger, hate, love, etc. Hence, this option is the correct one.
The hippocampus is that part of the limbic system that converts short-term memory to long-term memory. It does not regulate feelings and emotions; hence, this option is incorrect.

The thalamus serves as a relay station for sensory information. It also regulates motor control, consciousness and alertness, sleep cycle, and memory and learning. It does not partake in regulating emotions. Hence, this option is incorrect.

The hypothalamus helps in the coordination between the nervous system and the endocrine system. It also regulates the body's circadian rhythms. It is not involved in controlling or regulating emotions. So, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The amygdala is the part of the brain that is involved with emotions and feelings. It acts as the brain's emotional processing center, playing a crucial role in fear, pleasure, anxiety, and other emotions by attaching feelings to memories and sensations. Hence, option A is the correct option.

questions.text: Negative feedback mechanism is the characteristic of which class?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: While fish do not have regulatory mechanisms to maintain homeostasis, the negative feedback mechanism is not exclusive to Class fish.
Amphibians also have regulatory mechanisms, but again, the negative feedback mechanism is not specific to Class Amphibia.
Reptilia, like fish and amphibians, have regulatory mechanisms to maintain homeostasis, but he negative feedback mechanism is not a defining characteristic of Class Reptilia.
The negative feedback mechanism is a regulatory process commonly found in mammals.

questions.explanation: Negative feedback is a type of regulation in biological systems in which the end product of a process, in turn, reduces the stimulus of that same process. Feedback, in general, is a regulatory mechanism present in many biological reactions. By allowing certain pathways to be turned off and on, the body can control various aspects of its internal environment. Examples of negative feedback include the regulation of blood glucose levels and the temperature regulation of the body.

questions.text: The maximum enzymatic activity of pepsin is shown at _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: pH 2 is very acidic, and pepsin works best in a very acidic medium. Hence, pepsin works best at pH 2, and this option is the correct option.
pH 4 is acidic, but not too acidic for pepsin to show its maximum activity. Hence, this option is incorrect.
pH 6 is slightly acidic, and it is not the optimum pH for pepsin. Hence, this option is incorrect.
pH 8 is basic, but pepsin works best in acidic conditions. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Pepsin shows maximum activity in a strongly acidic medium. Hence, pH 2 is the optimum pH for pepsin. Option A is the correct option.

questions.text: Enzymes work by lowering the _ of the reactions they catalyze.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Kinetic energy refers to the energy of motion of molecules. While it plays a role in reactions, enzymes do not change the kinetic energy of the reactants. Instead, they primarily lower the activation energy needed for the reaction to proceed.

Heat energy can affect the rate of chemical reactions, but enzymes do not lower heat energy. Their role is to reduce the activation energy barrier, allowing reactions to occur more easily regardless of the temperature.

This is the correct answer. Activation energy is the minimum energy required to initiate a chemical reaction. Enzymes lower this energy barrier, enabling the reactants to transition to products more efficiently.

Potential energy pertains to the stored energy within molecules. While all chemical reactions involve changes in potential energy, enzymes specifically work by lowering the activation energy, not the potential energy itself.

questions.explanation: An enzyme binds to its specific substrate at its active site and facilitates the conversion of substrates into products. By lowering the activation energy, enzymes make it easier for reactions to occur. The incorrect options reflect common misconceptions:Kinetic energy: This refers to the energy of motion and does not relate directly to the role of enzymes in catalyzing reactions.Heat energy: While temperature can influence reaction rates, it is the activation energy that enzymes lower, not the heat energy.Potential energy: Though potential energy relates to the energy stored in molecules, enzymes do not directly lower potential energy; they lower activation energy, which is crucial for the reaction's initiation.Thus, activation energy is the correct answer as it directly correlates with the function of enzymes in biochemical reactions.

questions.text: _ reduces the enzyme productivity by blocking the substrate to enter the active site due to similar shape.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Competitive inhibitors are those that compete with the substrate in binding with the active site, due to similar structures. As it perfectly correlates with the given statement, this option is the correct option.

The non-competitive inhibitor binds at the allosteric site; a site that is separated from the active site. Thus, in non-competitive inhibition, the inhibitor can bind its target enzyme regardless of the presence of a bound substrate. Because the binding sites are different, there is no competition between the substrate and the inhibitor. Hence, this option is incorrect.

Coenzyme is defined as an organic molecule that binds to the active sites of certain enzymes to assist in the catalysis of a reaction. It is not related to the given statement, so this option is incorrect.
Enzyme activators are chemical compounds that increase the velocity of an enzymatic reaction. Their actions are opposite to the effect of enzyme inhibitors. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Competitive inhibitors are those that cause competition between substrate and inhibitor. There is a similarity between substrate and inhibitor, due to which the inhibitor can attach to the active site and block enzymatic activity. Hence, option A is the correct option.

questions.text: Among the oldest known fossils comes the _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Prokaryotes are believed to be the oldest known fossils ever discovered. It is also believed that they are the ancestors of all living organisms. Hence, this option is the correct option.
Amphibians are said to be evolved from fish. They are not the oldest known fossils. Hence, this option is incorrect.
Reptiles are said to be evolved from amphibians. They are not the oldest known fossils. Hence, this option is incorrect.
Fish are said to be evolved from earlier jawless fish. They are also not the oldest known fossils. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Prokaryotes are the oldest known organisms.They are about 3.5 million years ago. So it follows that the oldest fossils would be of prokaryotes as well. Option A is the correct option.

questions.text: Which of the following is not the component of bile?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Digestive enzymes are not a part of bile composition. Hence, this option is the correct one.
Bile contains bile salts. Hence, this option is incorrect.

Bile is a fluid produced by the liver that consists mainly of water, mucus, bile salts, cholesterol, bile pigments (like bilirubin), phospholipids, and electrolytes. Its main function is to aid in the digestion and absorption of fats and fat-soluble vitamins by emulsifying them in the small intestine. Hence, this option is incorrect.
Lecithin is a phospholipid. It is also present in bile. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Bile does not contain digestive enzymes. Instead, it contains bile salts and other substances that aid in the digestion and absorption of fats through a physical process called emulsification.

questions.text: Select the duration of cardiac cycle.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The cardiac cycle is longer than 0.6 seconds. Hence, this option is incorrect.
The cardiac cycle is longer than 0.4 seconds, so this option is incorrect.
The cardiac cycle typically lasts for 0.8 seconds; 0.4 seconds for diastole, 0.1 seconds for atrial systole, and 0.3 seconds for ventricular systole. Hence, this option is the correct option.
Cardiac cycle is longer than 0.7 seconds. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The cardiac cycle typically lasts for 0.8 seconds; 0.4 seconds for diastole, 0.1 seconds for atrial systole, and 0.3 seconds for ventricular systole. Option C is the correct option.

questions.text: The pathway of water transportation, in which water moves through plasmodesmata is _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The apoplast pathway of water is its transport through adjacent cell walls and intracellular spaces of plant tissues. Hence, this option is incorrect.
In the symplast pathway, water travels from one cell to another through plasmodesmata. Hence, this option is the correct option.
Vacuolar pathway water travels from one vacuole to another vacuole. Hence, this option is incorrect.
The ascent of sap in the xylem tissue of plants is the upward movement of water and minerals from the root to the aerial parts of the plant. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: In the symplast pathway, water travels from one cell to another through plasmodesmata. Hence, option B is the correct option.

questions.text: Enzyme trypsinogen is activated by _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Trypsinogen is activated in a basic medium, not an acidic one. Hence, this option is incorrect.
Pepsin is present in the stomach, whereas trypsinogen is activated in the small intestine. Hence, this option is incorrect.
The enzyme 'enterokinase' activates trypsinogen into its active form 'trypsin'. Hence, this option is the correct one.
Erypsin is not a recognised enzyme. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Enterokinase's primary function is to activate the digestion of proteins by converting pancreatic enzymes from their inactive forms into active ones. It achieves this by cleaving trypsinogen into its active form, trypsin. Once activated, trypsin goes on to activate other inactive pancreatic proenzymes like chymotrypsinogen, procarboxypeptidase, and proelastase.

questions.text: The area between the two lungs is called _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The periosteum is the sheath outside your bones that supplies them with blood, nerves and the cells that help them grow and heal. Hence, this option is incorrect.
Infudilbulum is the hollow stalk that connects the hypothalamus and the posterior pituitary gland. Hence, this option is incorrect.

The area between two lungs is called the mediastinum. The mediastinum is the central compartment of the thoracic cavity, situated between the two lungs. It contains vital structures such as the heart, major blood vessels, trachea, esophagus, thymus, and lymph nodes. This makes it an important area for protecting and supporting organs within the chest, so the option identifying it is correct.
Both lungs have a region called the hilum, which serves as the point of attachment between the lung root and the lung. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The area between two lungs is called the mediastinum. The organs in this area include the heart and its large blood vessels, the trachea, the esophagus, the thymus, and lymph nodes but not the lungs.

questions.text: All viruses are _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: An autotroph is an organism that can produce its food using light, water, carbon dioxide, or other chemicals. All viruses are not autotrophs, so this option is incorrect.
A heterotroph is an organism that eats other plants or animals for energy and nutrients. All viruses are not heterotrophs, so this option is incorrect.
Parasites are organisms that use other organisms for food, shelter, etc, and in exchange, they harm them. All viruses are parasites, so this option is the correct option.
An animal that naturally preys on others, is called a predator. All viruses are not predators, so this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Parasites are organisms that use other organisms for food, shelter, etc, and in exchange, they harm them. Viruses live and multiply in different cells, and in exchange, cause diseases. Hence, they fit in the definition of a parasite.

questions.text: Light independent phase of photosynthesis involves _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Hydrolysis of water, ATP generation, and production of reducing molecules are all part of the light-dependent phase of photosynthesis. Light independent phase involves the reaction of G3P to form energy-rich glucose, so option A is correct.
Hydrolysis of water occurs in the light-dependent phase of photosynthesis, not in the light-independent phase. So, this option is incorrect.
ATP generation occurs in the light-dependent phase of photosynthesis, not in the light-independent phase. So, this option is incorrect.
Production of reducing molecules occurs in the light-dependent phase of photosynthesis, not in the light-independent phase. So, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Hydrolysis of water, ATP generation, and production of reducing molecules are all part of the light-dependent phase of photosynthesis. Light independent phase involves the reaction of G3P to form energy-rich glucose so option A is correct.

questions.text: Select an example of a fibrous protein.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Enzymes are usually globular in structures, having achieved the tertiary or quarternary structure of proteins. They are not an example of fibrous proteins (primary or secondary structure), so this option is incorrect.

Fibrous proteins are those which have a spindle or rectangular shape. Usually, they have a secondary level of bonding and are known as structural proteins as well. Collagen is a structural, fibrous protein. So, this option is the correct option.
Hb, also known as haemoglobin protein, is a globular protein, not a fibrous protein. So, this option is incorrect.
All hormones are not proteins in nature. However, the proteinaceous hormones are globular proteins, not fibrous proteins. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Fibrous proteins are those which have a spindle or rectangular shape. Usually, they have a secondary level of bonding and are involved in maintaining structures. Options A, C, and D are all functional, globular proteins. Collagen is the only structural, fibrous protein. Hence, option B is correct.

questions.text: Three hydrophobic fatty acid tails and a glycerol molecule join to form a _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Monoglycerides are conventionally made from vegetable oils or animal fat through a lysis-reaction approach using alcohol from glycerol. Hence, this option is incorrect.
A diglyceride is an ester of glycerol joined with two fatty acid molecules through ester linkages. Hence, this option is incorrect.
When three fatty acids are involved in forming an ester bond with glycerol, a triglyceride is formed. Hence, this option is the correct option.
Phospholipid is made up of two fatty acid tails and a phosphate group head. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Esterification of alcohol with fatty acid results in the formation of acylglycerol, also called glyceride.When three fatty acids are involved in forming an ester bond with glycerol, a triglyceride is formed.

questions.text: Which property of water enables it to act as a temperature stabilizer in living organisms?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: High polarity alone doesn’t make water a temperature stabilizer, so this option is incorrect. Water’s temperature-stabilizing ability mainly comes from its high specific heat, not just its polarity.

Water isn’t non-polar. Its bent shape and uneven charge distribution make it strongly polar, which allows it to form hydrogen bonds. Because of this, the option describing water as non-polar is incorrect.

Water has a high specific heat capacity, which means that a large quantity of heat energy is required to bring a 1-degree Celsius change in temperature. So, it can absorb large amounts of heat easily. In this way, it stays cool while surrounding substances. For this reason, it is used as a temperature regulator. Hence, this option is the correct option.
The high heat of the vaporization of water does not account for its use as a temperature stabilizer. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Water has a high specific heat capacity, which means that a large quantity of heat energy is required to bring a 1-degree Celsius change in temperature. So, it can absorb large amounts of heat easily. In this way, it stays cool while surrounding substances. For this reason, it is used as a temperature regulator. Hence, option C is correct.

questions.text: Which of the following carbohydrates are sweet among all?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Monosaccharides are the simplest type of carbohydrates, and many of them have a naturally sweetest taste. So this option is correct.

Disaccharides are usually not as sweet as monosaccharides because they are made of two sugar units linked together, and their structure doesn’t activate sweetness receptors as strongly. So this option is incorrect.
Oligosaccharides are made of several linked sugar units and don’t taste as sweet as monosaccharides, so this option is incorrect.
Polysaccharides are large, complex carbohydrate chains and don’t have a sweet taste, so this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Monosaccharides are the simplest and the sweetest sugars. Option A is the correct option.

questions.text: Chemically, cell wall of fungi is made up of _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cellulose forms the main structure of plant cell walls, giving them rigidity and support. Fungal cell walls, however, are made of chitin, so this option is incorrect.

Lignin is one of the main components of the plant cell walls, and it is a natural phenolic polymer with high molecular weight, complex composition, and structure. It is not present in fungal cell wall. Hence, this option is incorrect.
The fungal cell wall is primarily made of chitin, a strong polymer that provides rigidity and protection. It also contains glucans and glycoproteins. Hence, this option is correct.
Murein is an important component of the bacterial cell wall and forms peptidoglycan there. It is not present in fungal cell walls. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The fungal cell wall is primarily made of chitin, a strong polymer that provides rigidity and protection. It also contains glucans and glycoproteins. Hence, this option is correct.

questions.text: The inner membrane of mitochondria is folded to form a finger like _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cisternae are flattened membrane-bound sacs found in the endoplasmic reticulum and Golgi apparatus, not foldings. Hence, this option is incorrect.

Cristae are the inward folds of the mitochondrial inner membrane, which increase the surface area for enzymes involved in cellular respiration. This structure enhances ATP production efficiency. Hence, this option is correct.
Chromatin is the uncondensed form of DNA found in the nucleus, involved in gene expression and DNA replication. Hence, this option is incorrect.

The inner membrane of a chloroplast is smooth and continuous, lacking the folds or invaginations seen in mitochondria (cristae). It encloses the stroma and helps maintain the internal environment for photosynthesis. Since it does not have invaginations, the statement suggesting otherwise is incorrect. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Cristae are the folded invagination of the mitochondrial inner membrane.

questions.text: Glycogen lipid droplet forms the most important _ of the cell.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cell organelles are specialized structures with a cell that perform specific functions. Glycogen lipid droplets are not cell organelles, so this option is incorrect.

Cell inclusions are non-living components within a cell that are temporarily or permanently present. They are not essential but serve various functions. Glycogen lipid droplets are also included in cell inclusions, so this option is correct.

Non-membraneous organelles are the structures within a cell that lack a membrane-bound structure. Glycogen lipid droplets are not non-membraneous as they are enclosed by a membrane, and they are also not an organelle. This option is incorrect.
Cell matrix is the non-cellular component present outside the cell. Glycogen lipid droplets are not included in the cell matrix, so this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Cell inclusions are non-living components within a cell that are temporarily or permanently present. They are not essential but serve various functions. Glycogen lipid droplets are also included in cell inclusions, so option B is correct.

questions.text: Lymphatic system contains all of the following organs except _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: They are part of the lymphatic system. So, the option is incorrect.
They are part of lymphatic system. So, the option is incorrect.
The spleen is a part of the lymphatic system hence it is the largest lymphatic organ. So, the option is incorrect.
They are not a part of the lymphatic system, rather they are included in the respiratory system. Hence, this option is the correct option.

questions.explanation: All options are part of the lymphatic system, except the lungs. Option D is the correct option.

questions.text: Transfer of genetic material from one bacteria to another through a third party, usually a bacteriophage, is called?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Conjugation is a process in bacteria where genetic material, usually plasmid DNA, is transferred from one cell to another via direct cell-to-cell contact, often using a pilus. It does not involve the formation of endospores or other structures. Hence, this option is incorrect.

Bacterial transformation is a type of horizontal gene transfer in which bacteria take up naked DNA from their environment and incorporate it into their genome. It does not involve reproduction or spore formation. Hence, this option is incorrect.

Transduction is a process of horizontal gene transfer in bacteria where a bacteriophage (virus) carries genetic material from one bacterium to another. This allows genes to move between bacteria without direct contact. Hence, this option is correct.

Transportation refers to the movement of molecules, ions, or other substances across a biological membrane, like the plasma membrane, using diffusion, osmosis, or transport proteins. It does not involve genetic material transfer. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Transduction is the process by which a virus transfers genetic material from one bacterium to another through a third party.

questions.text: Which of the following helps in developing immunity against germs?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Radiotherapy is not a means to develop immunity against germs, so the option is incorrect.
Chemotherapy is not a means to develop immunity against germs, so the option is incorrect.
Vaccination helps acquire immunity against germs. So, this option is correct.
Antibiotics are not a means to develop immunity against germs, so the option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Vaccination helps acquire immunity against germs.

questions.text: The best temperature for spermatogenesis is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is higher than the optimum temperature for spermatogenesis, so this option is incorrect.
It is lower than the optimum temperature for spermatogenesis, so this option is incorrect.
It is lower than the optimum temperature for spermatogenesis, so this option is incorrect.
35°C is the best temperature for spermatogenesis, so this option is the correct option.

questions.explanation: It is a fact that 35°C is the best temperature for spermatogenesis. Option D is the correct option.

questions.text: Leydig cells in testes are responsible for :
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Leydig cells function to stimulate the production of the hormone testosterone. This option is the correct option.
Leydig cells do not stimulate FSH production, so this option is incorrect.
Although sperm are produced in response to testosterone, Leydig cells do not cause it directly. So, this option is incorrect.
Leydig cells cause testosterone secretion and do not inhibit it. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Leydig cells function to stimulate the production of the hormone testosterone.

questions.text: _ is the inflammation of joints.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Joint inflammation is also termed osteoarthritis. The word ITIS is used to determine inflammation. This option is the correct option.
Osteoporosis refers to the weakening of bones. It is not the inflammation of bones, so the option is incorrect.
Arteriosclerosis refers to the thickening and hardening of arteries. It is not the inflammation of bones, so the option is incorrect.
Osteosclerosis refers to the hardening of bones or increased bone tissue density. It is not the inflammation of bones, so the option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Joint inflammation is also known as osteoarthritis. The word ITIS is used to determine inflammation. Option A is the correct option.

questions.text: What is the function of myoglobin?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Myoglobin stores oxygen in muscles; hence, this option is correct.
Myoglobin does not store carbon dioxide, so the option is incorrect.
Myoglobin can only store oxygen, and not transport it. So, the option is incorrect.
Myoglobin can neither store nor transport carbon dioxide, so the option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Myoglobin stores oxygen in muscles. Hence, option A is the correct option.

questions.text: These are the genes which tend to be inherited together and do not assort independently.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Linked genes are so close to each other (lie on the same chromosome) that during division, they are not separated. As a result, they are passed on to the next generation together. This option is the correct option.
Dependent genes is another term for linked genes. However, due to the presence of the option 'linked genes', this option will be marked incorrect.
Recombinant geners are artificially modified genes through genetic engineering. They may or may not segregate, so the option is incorrect.
Independent genes are the unlinked genes that are located on different chromosomes, and they may or may not be inherited together. This option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Linked genes are so close to each other, that during division they are not separated, and as a result, are passed on to the next generation together.

questions.text: The molecules of iodine form the _.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Molecular crystals are substances that have relatively weak intermolecular binding, such as dry ice (solidified carbon dioxide), solid forms of noble gases (e.g., argon, krypton, and xenon), and crystals of numerous organic compounds. Iodine crystals are molecular crystals, so the option is correct.
Ionic crystals are crystals in which atoms form ionic bonds with one another. Iodine crystals are not ionic crystals, so the option is incorrect.
Covalent crystals are crystals in which each atom forms covalent bonds with each other. Iodine crystals are not covalent crystals, so the option is incorrect.
Metallic crystals consist of metal cations surrounded by a "sea" of mobile valence electrons. Iodine crystals are not metallic crystals, so the option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Iodine is a molecular crystal due to its non-polar nature.

questions.text: For the chemical reactionN2(g) +3H2(g) ->2NH3(g), we can maximize the yield by
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The reaction is exothermic, so by increasing the temperature, the reaction will move in reverse direction. As a result, the yeild will become less. The option is incorrect.
If we decrease the pressure, the reaction will lean toward the reverse direction. It will lessen the product, so the option is incorrect.

options.text: By increasing volume of reaction vessel
options.explanation: If we would increase the volume, the reaction will move in the backward direction, decreasing the yield. This option is incorrect.

options.text: By continous withdrawl of ammonia after intervals
options.explanation: Continual withdrawal of NH3 shifts the equilibrium to the right side to balance the effect of decreasing the amount of NH3 (Le Chatelier principle). Hence, this option is correct.

questions.explanation: Continual withdrawal of NH3 shifts the equilibrium to the right side to balance the effect of decreasing the amount of NH3 (Le Chatelier principle). Hence, option D is correct.

questions.text: By which of the following factors equilibrium state is attained earlier?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: While temperature can affect the rate of a reaction, it does not necessarily lead to an earlier attainment of the equilibrium state. Temperature influences the kinetic energy of the molecules, affecting the reaction rate, but it does not change the position of equilibrium. Equilibrium is reached when the forward and backward reactions occur at the same rate, regardless of temperature.

Similar to temperature, pressure affects the reaction rate but not the equilibrium state. Changing the pressure alters the concentration of reactant molecules and, consequently, the reaction rate. However, equilibrium is defined by the ratio of concentrations, not the absolute pressure.

Concentrations do play a role in establishing the equilibrium state. However, the equilibrium state is not necessarily reached earlier by changing concentrations alone. Altering the concentrations of reactants affects the rate at which equilibrium is attained, but it does not guarantee a faster attainment.

Catalysts are substances that increase the rate of a reaction without being consumed in the process. They provide an alternative pathway with lower activation energy. By reducing the energy barrier, catalysts speed up both the forward and backward reactions, allowing the equilibrium state to be reached more quickly. The presence of a catalyst does not alter the position of equilibrium but helps achieve equilibrium faster by facilitating the conversion of reactants to products and vice versa.

questions.explanation: Only using a catalyst can help attain equilibrium earlier as it speeds up the rate of both forward and backward reactions. This enhances the rate at which equilibrium is established.

questions.text: What is incorrect about the activated complex?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Activated complexes are intermediate high-energy species formed during a reaction. A high-energy species would need higher activated energy for the formation of an activated species, which cannot be easily reached by the system.

An activated complex is an unstable arrangement of atoms that exists momentarily at the peak of the activation energy barrier. Because of its high energy, the activated complex exists for an extremely short period (about 10−13s).

The activated complex is temporary and unstable because the energy is more elevated than the products or reactants. An activated complex breaks down into reactants if there isn't sufficient energy for the complex to result in products. The products are only formed if there is sufficient energy available.

options.text: Potential energy is maximum at activated complex stage
options.explanation: An activated complex is an unstable molecule that possesses maximum potential energy. It may be reversed and become a reactant again. It shows the intermediate states which possess the highest energy. It can be considered as the intermediate structure which can be formed in a chemical reaction.

questions.explanation: The activated complex is temporary and unstable because the energy is more elevated than the products or reactants. An activated complex breaks down into reactants if there isn't sufficient energy for the complex to result in products. The products are only formed if there is sufficient energy available.

questions.text: If a reaction is first order with respect to a reactant then the rate will be —--- if the concentration of the reactant is doubled.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: When a reaction is said to be first order with respect to a reactant, it means that the rate of the reaction is directly proportional to the concentration of that reactant raised to the power of one.Mathematically, if we have a first-order reaction with respect to reactant A, the rate equation would be expressed as:rate = k[A]^1Where [A] represents the concentration of reactant A, and k is the rate constantNow, if the concentration of reactant A is doubled, the new concentration would be 2[A]. Substituting this into the rate equation:new rate = k[2A]^1Since 2A is twice the concentration of A, we can simplify the equation as follows:new rate = 2k[A]^1As we can see, the new rate is twice the original rate. Therefore, if the concentration of the reactant is doubled, the rate will also be doubled. This is why option A is correct.

If the concentration of the reactant is doubled, the rate would not be halved. As shown in the correct explanation above, the rate is directly proportional to the concentration, so doubling the concentration doubles the rate.
This option is incorrect because the rate does not decrease by a factor of one fourth. Again, doubling the concentration leads to doubling the rate.
This option is incorrect as well. Doubling the concentration does not result in quadrupling the rate. It only doubles the rate, as shown in the correct explanation.

questions.explanation: When a reaction is said to be first order with respect to a reactant, it means that the rate of the reaction is directly proportional to the concentration of that reactant raised to the power of one.Mathematically, if we have a first-order reaction with respect to reactant A, the rate equation would be expressed as:rate = k[A]^1Where [A] represents the concentration of reactant A, and k is the rate constantNow, if the concentration of reactant A is doubled, the new concentration would be 2[A]. Substituting this into the rate equation:new rate = k[2A]^1Since 2A is twice the concentration of A, we can simplify the equation as follows:new rate = 2k[A]^1As we can see, the new rate is twice the original rate. Therefore, if the concentration of the reactant is doubled, the rate will also be doubled. This is why option A is correct.

questions.text: One calorie is equal to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. One calorie is equal to 4.18 joules (J), not kilojoules (KJ). Since there are 1000 joules in a kilojoule, this option overestimates the conversion.
1 Calorie=4.18 joules

his option is incorrect. The given value is incorrect in two ways. First, the correct conversion factor is 4.18 joules per calorie, not kilojoules. Second, the given value includes the unit "mol-1," which suggests a molar quantity. However, the conversion factor between calories and joules is not dependent on the molar amount of a substance.

questions.text: Ionization energy decreases down the group because:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Shielding does not remain constant but rather increases down the group, contributing to the decrease in ionization energy.

This option is incorrect. The atomic radius increases down a group. As you move down the periodic table, each subsequent element has one additional electron shell. The addition of electron shells leads to an increase in atomic radius. The increase in atomic radius results in a greater distance between the nucleus and the outermost electrons, reducing the attractive force and making it easier to remove an electron. Consequently, the increase in atomic radius down the group contributes to the decrease in ionization energy.

This option is incorrect. Proton number refers to the number of protons in an atom's nucleus, which increases down a group. However, ionization energy decreases down the group despite the increase in proton number. The increase in proton number does not have a significant effect on the ionization energy because the electrons being removed are in the same principal energy level and are similarly shielded by inner electron shells.

Ionization energy is the energy needed to remove an electron from its atom. Down the group, Atomic radius as well as shielding increases, as a result, the attraction forces between electrons and nucleus reduces. Hence, less energy is required for electrons surrounding the nucleus to be removed from the atom i.e. ionization energy.

questions.text: The type of bonding in Zinc is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ionic bonding involves the transfer of electrons from one atom to another, resulting in the formation of positive and negative ions that attract each other. In the case of zinc, it does not readily form Zn2+ ions (positive ions) and participates in typical ionic bonding. Instead, it primarily forms metallic bonds. So, this option is incorrect.

Covalent bonding occurs when atoms share electrons to achieve a stable electron configuration. While zinc can form covalent bonds in certain compounds, such as zinc oxide (ZnO), the predominant bonding in pure zinc is metallic. So, this option is incorrect.
Dative or coordinate covalent bonding involves one atom providing both electrons for a shared pair. This type of bonding is not commonly observed in pure zinc. So, this option is incorrect.

Zinc is a metal. All metals exhibit metallic bonding between their atoms. Metallic bonding occurs between metal atoms and is characterized by the sharing of valence electrons among a sea of delocalized electrons. In metallic bonds, the valence electrons are not tightly held by individual atoms but are free to move throughout the entire metal structure. This option is correct.

questions.explanation: Zinc is a metal. All metals exhibit metallic bonding between their atoms. Zinc is a transition metal that exhibits a fixed valency of +2 in most of its compounds. It does not commonly show variable valency like other transition elements. For example, zinc oxide (ZnO) and zinc sulfate (ZnSO4) both involve zinc with a valency of +2. So, this option is correct.

Chromium is a transition metal that commonly shows variable valency. It can have valencies ranging from -2 to +6, with the most stable and commonly observed valencies being +2, +3, and +6. For example, in chromium(III) chloride (CrCl3), chromium exhibits a valency of +3.

questions.text: In alkane each carbon atom has hybridization _.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: “sp3 hybridization” refers to the mixing character of one 2s-orbital and three 2p-orbitals to create four hybrid orbitals with similar characteristics. For an atom to be sp3 hybridized, it must have an s orbital and three p orbitals. The carbon atoms in alkanes are sp3-hybridized, and have tetrahedral shapes, with the bonded atoms at angles of 109.5° to each other. Free rotation is possible around the carbon-carbon single bonds in alkanes, making the carbon chains very flexible and "floppy" — particularly for the larger molecules.

sp hybridization is observed when one s and one p orbital in the same main shell of an atom mix to form two new equivalent orbitals. The new orbitals formed are called sp hybridized orbitals. It forms linear molecules with an angle of 180°.

The sp2 hybridization is the mixing of one s and two p atomic orbitals, which involves the promotion of one electron in the s orbital to one of the 2p atomic orbitals. The combination of these atomic orbitals creates three new hybrid orbitals equal in energy-level.

A carbon atom is sp2 hybridized when bonding takes place between 1 s-orbital with two p orbitals. There is a formation of two single bonds and one double bond between three atoms. The hybrid orbitals are placed in a triangular arrangement with 120° angles between bonds.

questions.explanation: In an alkane, each carbon atom has sp3 hybridization. This means that one 2s-orbital and three 2p-orbitals mix to create four hybrid orbitals. These hybrid orbitals have a tetrahedral shape with bonded atoms at angles of 109.5°. Free rotation is possible around the carbon-carbon single bonds, making alkane chains flexible. Options B, C, and D describe different types of hybridization, but they are not applicable to the hybridization of carbon atoms in alkanes.

questions.text: Which of the the following has highest surface tension?

options.explanation: Benzene is a nonpolar compound composed of carbon and hydrogen atoms. It lacks the ability to form hydrogen bonds. Consequently, the cohesive forces in benzene are weaker than in water, resulting in a lower surface tension.

The term "alcohol" encompasses a wide range of organic compounds with different molecular structures. However, in general, alcohols tend to have lower surface tension compared to water. While some alcohols can form hydrogen bonds, they typically have fewer hydrogen bonding sites than water molecules, leading to weaker cohesive forces and lower surface tension.

Ether is another organic compound that typically exhibits lower surface tension than water. Like benzene, ethers lack the ability to form hydrogen bonds to the same extent as water. Consequently, the cohesive forces between ether molecules are weaker, resulting in lower surface tension.

Water has the highest surface tension among the options listed. This is primarily because water molecules can form strong hydrogen bonds with each other. Hydrogen bonding occurs when the hydrogen atom of one water molecule is attracted to the oxygen atom of another water molecule. These hydrogen bonds contribute to the cohesive forces between water molecules and result in relatively high surface tension.

Structural proteins are involved in providing support and structure to cells, tissues, and organs. They contribute to the physical integrity and shape of various biological structures. Thyroxine, also known as T4, is not primarily involved in structural functions but rather acts as a hormone.

Transport proteins are responsible for carrying molecules, ions, or other substances across cellular membranes or throughout the body. While thyroxine does bind to transport proteins in the bloodstream for distribution, its primary function is not related to transport but rather as a hormone.

Amplification refers to the process of increasing the amplitude or strength of a signal. It is commonly used in electronic circuits to increase the power or voltage of a signal. However, amplification is not specifically related to the conversion of alternating current into direct current.

Half wave rectification is a process in which only one half of the alternating current (AC) waveform is converted into direct current (DC). It involves using a diode to block or eliminate the negative half cycles of the AC waveform, allowing only the positive half cycles to pass through and convert into DC. The result is an output waveform that consists of a series of positive pulses or half cycles.
